title: Death, inequality, and the pandemic in the nation's capital.
abstract: Abrupt changes in mortality rates and life expectancy provide critical insights into how crises like COVID-19 exacerbate health inequalities across demographic groups. Washington, DC, a city marked by substantial socioeconomic and racial diversity, is a valuable case study to examine the pandemic's impact. Data on all-cause mortality, cause-specific mortality, and life expectancy highlight how pre-existing disparities have widened across sociodemographic groups relative to pre-pandemic levels.
